title: Permission denied due to SELINUX settings
symptom: The error 'EACCES: permission denied, scandir' occurs when trying to access the credentials directory. This prevents n8n from functioning correctly.
applies_when: Running n8n on a Fedora system
root_cause: SELINUX can restrict access to files and directories based on security policies. In this case, it was preventing n8n from accessing the credentials directory, leading to permission errors.
fix_steps: Run the command 'setenforce 0' to temporarily disable SELINUX
Consider configuring SELINUX policies for a permanent solution
agent_instruction: Temporarily disable SELINUX to resolve the permission denied error.
safety_notes: Disabling SELINUX can expose the system to security risks; consider configuring it properly instead.
